title: Launcher console encoding: 3-mode policy for interactive + piped + UTF-8 terminals
desc: Set Console.OutputEncoding per mode so multi-byte chars (Korean CP949) render correctly everywhere: redirected streams force UTF-8 (inter-process standard), UTF-8 terminal keeps UTF-8, CP949 conhost uses CP949 with ? fallback. Explicit StreamWriter needed for redirected streams because .NET defaults to OS ACP.
steps: Detect mode triad: (1) stdout/stderr redirected -> UTF-8 (pipe consumer standard); (2) UTF-8 term (MSYSTEM/TERM env or CP65001) -> UTF-8; (3) CP949 conhost -> CP949 with ReplacementFallback (? for missing chars like em-dash)

Redirected streams need explicit StreamWriter wrap: Console.SetOut(new StreamWriter(OpenStandardOutput(), Encoding.UTF8){AutoFlush=true}) -- Console.OutputEncoding alone doesn't apply to pipe-redirected streams
Korean chars survive all three modes: UTF-8 passthrough or CP949 multi-byte encoding are both native
Em-dash and box-drawing have no CP949 mapping -> replace in source with ASCII (bulk wkedit); ReplacementFallback is last-resort safety
Never call SetConsoleOutputCP -- mutates terminal's CP and breaks the host shell after our process exits
author: claude
